recap: Expressions, equations, identities, and formulas all have different meanings in algebra, and you can only add or subtract like terms — those with the same letter and power.

questions.question: Write down the coefficient of x in the expression 7x squared - 4x + 9.

steps: The term with just x (not x squared) is -4x.

questions.question:
State whether each of the following is an expression, equation, or identity.
(a) 3x + 5 = 14
(b) 4(x + 2) = 4x + 8
(c) 2x squared - x + 7

steps: (a) Has an equals sign and is only true for one value of x — this is an equation.
(b) Has an equals sign and is true for all values of x — this is an identity.
(c) Has no equals sign — this is an expression.

questions.question: A triangle has sides of length (2x + 3), (x + 5), and (4x - 1). Write a simplified expression for the perimeter.

steps: Group like terms: (5x squared - 2x squared) + (3x - x) + 7.

questions.question: A cuboid has a length of (3x - 1), a width of x, and a height of 2. Write a simplified expression for the total surface area. (Surface area of a cuboid = 2lw + 2lh + 2wh.)

summary: An expression has no equals sign; an equation has one and is true for specific values; an identity is true for all values.
The coefficient is the number multiplying the variable; the constant is the term with no variable.
Only like terms (same variable and same power) can be added or subtracted.
a squared means a x a — it is not the same as 2a.
